_comment: Ogle offline demo — DRIFTED signatures. Same graph as healthy-signatures.json. Two serving-path failures, deliberately different in character: (1) dbt.orders breaks LOUDLY — SCHEMA (order_total DOUBLE -> STRING), VOLUME (row count collapsed 1.25M -> 40K), QUALITY (order_total null 0.001 -> 0.42); (2) dbt.churn_features drifts SILENTLY — same schema, same 312K rows, same null rates, but its value distributions moved out from under the model: DISTRIBUTION (customer_id uniqueness 100% -> 55%, a fan-out join now duplicating keys), MEAN (monthly_charges 64.8 -> 88.2, +36% covariate shift), STDEV (tenure_days spread 420 -> 90, collapsed), RANGE (num_support_calls max 12 -> 45, a handful of overflow rows the mean/stdev never feel). customers is unchanged, so the alert stays scoped to the real blast radius. Together the two tables exercise 7 of Ogle's 8 drift dimensions in one keyless run (freshness is opt-in/clock-driven, out of scope for a static fixture).
